channel.title: Booked Job — for the trades
channel.description: Real talk for service pros — more booked jobs, better pricing, getting paid, and dodging the lead-reseller traps.

item.title: Is Angi Worth It for Contractors? The Real Cost Per Booked Job
item.description: Is Angi worth it? The honest math: leads get shared with up to 12 pros, so your real cost per booked job runs ~$1,430–2,500 — vs ~$290 organic. Here's the full breakdown, plus a free calculator that shows YOUR real cost per Angi lead.

item.title: How to Price a Home-Service Job (Without Leaving Money on the Table)
item.description: Most contractors don't lose money on the work — they lose it on the estimate. The fix: price off margin, not markup (a 50% markup is only a 33% margin). Here's the money math nobody teaches you in the apprenticeship, plus a free calculator for what to actually charge.

item.title: How to Get More Google Reviews for Your Service Business
item.description: Reviews decide who shows up in the map pack and who gets the call. The system: ask in person the second the job's done, then text the direct review link within the hour — one tap. Ask every customer (gating is against Google's rules), and aim for a steady trickle, not a burst.

item.title: Surviving the Slow Season: A Cash-Flow Playbook for the Trades
item.description: The slow season is won in the busy season. Bank a 3–6 month cushion while work flows, sell maintenance plans and weather-independent jobs, re-engage past customers, and INCREASE marketing while competitors go quiet. The shops that struggle assume every good month lasts forever.
